title: Java Method Return
description: Practice a static method that returns a value.

choices: Compilation fails because add is not public
completionChecks.explanation: add returns the int sum a + b, so add(2, 3) is 5; a static method need not be public to be called within the same class.

completionChecks.prompt: What does the static keyword on add allow?
choices: Calling add through the class, such as Calculator.add(2, 3), without an instance
Returning a different type on each call
completionChecks.explanation: static binds add to the class rather than an instance, so callers invoke Calculator.add(2, 3) without creating a Calculator object.

completionChecks.prompt: Inside Calculator, what does System.out.println(add(4, -1)) print?
choices: Compilation fails because add is static
completionChecks.explanation: The method returns the int sum a + b, so 4 plus -1 is 3; static does not prevent calling it from class code.
